sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NEA: Looking At The Positives, And A Future Concern
feed.summary: The Nuveen AMT-Free Quality Municipal Income Fund (NEA) experienced a significant Q4 rally and increased income, benefiting investors. While this boost is positive for short-term holders, its sustainability is questionable, prompting caution for long-term investors. A favorable macroeconomic environment with lower inflation suggests that the Federal Reserve is unlikely to raise interest rates further, which is generally positive for bonds, including municipal bonds like those held by NEA.

feed.title: Arm shares edge higher as Nvidia sells entire stake in company
feed.summary: Nvidia has sold its entire stake of 1.1 million shares in British semiconductor firm Arm, valued at $155.8 million, as revealed in a recent SEC filing. Despite this divestment, Arm's New York-listed shares rose over 1%, and the two companies maintain a strong relationship through a 20-year licensing agreement. Nvidia's exit follows its failed 2022 bid to acquire Arm for $40 billion, and Arm recently reported better-than-expected fiscal third-quarter sales, driven by AI project momentum.

feed.title: Analog Devices: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) reported a strong fiscal first quarter, with profits of $830.8 million and adjusted earnings of $2.46 per share, exceeding Wall Street expectations. The semiconductor maker also surpassed revenue forecasts, bringing in $3.16 billion, and provided optimistic guidance for the fiscal second quarter. ADI shares have performed well, rising 24% year-to-date and 57% over the last 12 months.

feed.title: From Rides To Robots: Uber’s Path Through The AV And AI Era (NYSE:UBER)
feed.summary: Uber Technologies delivered strong 2024 results, with revenue up 18% to $52B and normalized EPS rising 82% to $2.46/share. The company's hybrid model, leveraging both autonomous vehicles (AVs) and human drivers, and the Uber One loyalty program, along with accelerating advertising revenues, are enhancing monetization and operational resilience. However, long-term risks include potential disintermediation from AVs and agentic AI, which could impact Uber's aggregator advantage.

feed.title: eBay Earnings on Deck – Stock Higher Into The Print
feed.summary: eBay (EBAY) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings after market close on February 18, 2026, with analysts expecting $1.33 EPS and $2.73 billion in revenue. The stock, currently at $82.33, is up 2.98% on the day but down 19% from its 52-week high, reflecting investor focus on whether its platform transformation strategy can drive sustainable advertising revenue growth and improve margins. The primary driver for post-earnings stock movement will be management's guidance for 2026, particularly regarding advertising revenue trajectory and margin expectations.

feed.title: Fitness Wearable Boom Drives Record Profits For Garmin
feed.summary: Garmin reported record-breaking fourth-quarter 2025 results, with significant revenue growth in its fitness segment, driven by strong demand for advanced wearables. The company surpassed analyst estimates for both revenue and adjusted EPS, and announced plans to increase its quarterly dividend and initiate a new share repurchase program. Garmin also provided a strong outlook for 2026, expecting higher revenue and adjusted EPS than current analyst consensus.

feed.title: BofA Securities Cuts Price Target on Cadence Design Systems to $375 From $400, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: BofA Securities has lowered its price target for Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) to $375 from $400, while continuing to recommend a "Buy" rating for the stock. This adjustment comes despite Cadence Design Systems reporting an earnings beat in Q4 and being viewed as a beneficiary of AI demand. Other analysts like Piper Sandler, Morgan Stanley, Berenberg Bank, and Loop Capital also adjusted their price targets for Cadence, with varying rating changes.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share for $425
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL), recently purchased 1 share of TPL common stock for $425.55. Following this transaction, Horizon Kinetics directly holds over 3.4 million shares. The stock is currently trading slightly above the purchase price, and InvestingPro data suggests it may be in overbought territory.

feed.title: From gym floor to global stage: Built For Athletes powers up with NatWest’s £1m growth investment
feed.summary: NatWest has provided Warrington-based Built For Athletes with a £1.025 million funding package to support its global expansion and sustainability goals. The company, founded in 2018 by Nicholas Costello, specializes in design-protected backpacks for fitness enthusiasts and has partnered with global brands like Alpine F1 and Borussia Dortmund FC. The investment, comprising a trade loan and invoice finance, will enable product development, operational capability, and international growth.

feed.title: Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) Stock: Dips After New Psoriasis-Obesity Trial Data
feed.summary: Eli Lilly's stock saw a 1.65% decline to $1,018.92 following the announcement of positive Phase 3b TOGETHER-PsO trial results. The trial demonstrated that a combination of Taltz and Zepbound significantly improved skin clearance and weight loss in patients with psoriasis and obesity compared to Taltz alone. Despite the clinical success, which offers a comprehensive treatment for these comorbid conditions, the market reacted with a short-term dip in the company's share price.

feed.title: Pfizer Targets Real-World Myeloma Data in Taiwan With New Elranatamab Study
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. is initiating a new real-world observational study in Taiwan to evaluate the effectiveness of its multiple myeloma drug, elranatamab, in a routine hospital environment. This study aims to collect data on actual patient outcomes and treatment patterns, supporting the drug's longer-term acceptance and market share in Asia. While the study is not yet recruiting, its progress indicates Pfizer's commitment to its oncology portfolio, with potential positive impacts on pricing and reimbursement discussions in the future.

feed.title: Rail News - CSX trained thousands of first responders in 2025. For Railroad Career Professionals
feed.summary: CSX trained 5,645 first responders in 2025 through 75 incident training sessions focused on railroad-related emergencies. A significant enhancement to their Responder Incident Training (RIT) program was the introduction of a dedicated training locomotive, allowing for realistic, hands-on learning experiences. The company aims to prepare first responders to protect communities, employees, and the rail network during emergencies.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive’s Pension Class Deal Nabs Lawyers $96 Million
feed.summary: Lawyers representing retirees of Colgate-Palmolive Co. have been awarded over $96 million in attorneys' fees from a $332 million class settlement. This settlement addresses allegations that the consumer product maker shortchanged pension benefits. A federal judge deemed the 29% fee reasonable given the case's complexity and duration, also approving $2.9 million in litigation expenses and a $10,000 service award for the lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Technology at the service of the longevity revolution
feed.summary: Spain faces the significant challenge of an aging population and increasing dependency costs, which could overwhelm the social protection system. The article proposes leveraging technological innovation to transform dependency into autonomy for older adults, focusing on training and inclusive design to mitigate the digital divide. By using solutions like AI-powered monitoring, predictive telecare, and domestic robots, technology can help improve care, reduce the burden on caregivers, and ensure a better quality of life for the elderly.

feed.title: Payments Power Play: MA or AXP, Who Has the Deeper Moat in 2026?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Mastercard (MA) and American Express (AXP) to determine which company possesses a deeper competitive moat in 2026. Mastercard's asset-light network model and global diversification are highlighted as key strengths, contrasting with American Express's closed-loop model and direct lending exposure. While both companies are strong contenders in the digital payments landscape, Mastercard is considered better positioned for durable upside due to its stability, lower risk profile, and robust value-added services.

feed.title: Trimble sponsors engineering student competition
feed.summary: Trimble has announced a two-year sponsorship for the Engineering for People Design Challenge, an initiative led by Engineers Without Borders UK and South Africa. This competition tasks university students with developing sustainable engineering solutions for underserved communities, with the 2025/2026 challenge focusing on Ladywood in Birmingham, England. Trimble's expanded support builds on its history of contributions to Engineers Without Borders, aiming to foster critical skills in ethical decision-making, cultural sensitivity, and community engagement among future engineers.

feed.title: NCLH’s Sommer ‘excited’ for retirement, cruise expert weighs in
feed.summary: Harry Sommer, former President and CEO of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd (NCLH), has retired "suddenly" after the company's board appointed John W. Chidsey as his successor with immediate effect. Sommer expressed excitement for his retirement on LinkedIn, focusing on family and personal passions, while luxury cruise veteran Larry Pimentel noted that such immediate exits often stem from performance-based reasons, with the board losing confidence in leadership's ability to create shareholder value. Pimentel interpreted the board's language as indicating a need for a "turnaround specialist" to "sharpen execution, improve performance, strengthen financial performance, reduce leverage."

feed.title: Dr Reddy’s boosts gynaecology portfolio, acquires HRT brands from Mercury Pharma for $32.2 mn
feed.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories has acquired the trademarks Progynova and CycloProgynova and related assets in India from Mercury Pharma Group Ltd for $32.15 million. This acquisition strengthens Dr Reddy’s gynaecology portfolio and marks its entry into the hormone replacement therapy (HRT) segment, with Progynova being a leading brand in its category in India. The deal is expected to enhance Dr Reddy's presence in premium gynaecology therapies.

feed.title: Why Alphabet’s GOOG (Class C) stock is moving today: Fed minutes, Waymo scrutiny, YouTube glitch
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) saw a slight increase ahead of the Federal Reserve's January meeting minutes release. The company is facing scrutiny over its self-driving Waymo unit, addressed a brief YouTube outage, and secured a geothermal power deal for its Nevada operations, while the broader tech sector grapples with high AI buildout costs and potential shifts in interest rate expectations.

feed.title: 3 Discount Retail Stocks to Watch as Earnings Put Valuations to the Test
feed.summary: This article examines three discount retail stocks—Dollar Tree (DLTR), Ross Stores (ROST), and TJX Companies (TJX)—that investors should watch as the earnings season approaches, particularly given sustained inflation and shifting consumer spending habits. While these companies show varying degrees of recent performance and analyst sentiment, all face valuation concerns despite their growth potential. The author probes whether upcoming earnings reports will justify their current market valuations.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: OptimumBank Q4 2025 earnings beat estimates
feed.summary: OptimumBank Holdings Inc. reported record-breaking performance in Q4 2025, with EPS of $0.21, significantly surpassing the forecast of $0.15. The bank achieved a net income of $4.85 million and grew total assets to $1.11 billion, driven by strong loan and deposit growth, strategic expansions, and the upcoming launch of a new HUD/FHA financing subsidiary. Executives expressed confidence in continued growth, aiming for over 25% loan growth and $50 million in net interest income, supported by their disciplined relationship-driven lending model and infrastructure readiness.

feed.title: J&J fleshes out US investment plan, telegraphing $1B cell therapy plant and 500 new jobs in PA
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson plans to invest over $1 billion in a new cell therapy production plant in Montgomery County, Pennsylvania, creating 500 permanent biomanufacturing jobs and 4,000 construction jobs. This investment is part of J&J's broader $55 billion U.S. expansion strategy aimed at addressing potential pharmaceutical tariffs and boosting domestic production. The new facility will support the growing demand for cell therapies like Carvykti, which already exceeded blockbuster status last year and has previously faced supply constraints.

feed.title: GE HealthCare, BARDA ink $35M expansion to AI-enabled imaging deal
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has secured a $35 million contract expansion from the U.S. government's Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority (BARDA) to further develop AI-enabled ultrasound technology for trauma care. This builds on a prior 2023 agreement and aims to create tools that allow non-expert users to more easily and quickly diagnose patients, particularly in demanding environments. The expanded project focuses on improving assessments of lung pathologies and intra-abdominal injuries, with GE HealthCare co-funding the initiative, although BARDA provides the majority of the funds.

feed.title: Occidental earnings up next as oil recovery meets lower estimates
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum is set to report its Q4 earnings with lower estimates after divesting its chemicals business, OxyChem, to Berkshire Hathaway. Investors are focusing on the company's capital allocation priorities, including share buybacks and dividend growth, and its ability to generate free cash flow in a volatile oil market. Despite a recent recovery in oil prices, the fourth quarter's results will reveal how the company's streamlined operations performed.

feed.title: Federal Trade Commission Finalizes Consent Order In Boeing-Spirit Deal
feed.summary: The Federal Trade Commission has finalized a consent order for Boeing's acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems, addressing antitrust concerns by requiring Boeing to divest certain Spirit operations and maintain support for rivals. Concurrently, Air Astana finalized an order for up to 15 Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners, marking its largest single aircraft purchase. Boeing's shares were slightly down following these announcements, trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy earnings up next: Texas growth story in focus By Investing.com
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts expecting 45 cents per share on $2.25 billion in revenue. Investors will be scrutinizing the Houston-based utility's progress on its ambitious $65 billion 10-year capital plan, its load growth pipeline driven by Texas's economic expansion, and the effectiveness of its grid resiliency efforts. Despite a slight decline in EPS estimates, the company's long-term growth trajectory and favorable positioning for lower regulatory risk are key focuses.

feed.title: FDA agrees to review Moderna’s mRNA flu vaccine application in a reversal
feed.summary: The FDA has reversed an earlier decision and agreed to review Moderna's experimental mRNA flu shot application, clearing the way for potential availability for the upcoming influenza season. Moderna proposed a revised regulatory approach seeking full approval for adults 50 to 64 and accelerated approval for those 65 and up, which helped address the FDA's previous concerns about the trial's comparator vaccine. This development is crucial for Moderna's combination Covid-flu jab and its financial goals.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz replaces North American president with Kellanova exec
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz has appointed former Kellanova leader Nicolas Amaya as president of its North American business unit, replacing Pedro Navio. This move comes as new CEO Steve Cahillane, also from Kellanova, aims to revitalize the company after pausing an intended corporate breakup. Amaya will be crucial in implementing Cahillane's strategy to invest in marketing and product superiority to drive growth and improve the company's declining net sales.

feed.title: Uber Technologies (UBER) Stock: Surges 2.7% as $100M Robotaxi Charging Push Gains Momentum
feed.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) saw its stock surge by 2.7% following the announcement of a $100 million investment to expand autonomous vehicle charging hubs. This strategic move aims to strengthen Uber's position in the robotaxi and EV charging sectors, with a focus on building fast-charging stations in key U.S. cities like the Bay Area, Los Angeles, and Dallas. The company plans to roll out robotaxi services in at least 10 cities by the end of 2026, leveraging partnerships with various autonomous vehicle and charging operators to scale its self-driving operations efficiently.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher boosts connected planning with Oracle Cloud EPM
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific, a global leader in life sciences, has adopted Oracle Fusion Cloud Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) to enhance its enterprisewide planning. This move aims to address challenges stemming from significant growth, including managing diverse teams, supporting a large global sales channel, and overcoming data management issues from disparate systems. By centralizing data and improving collaboration with Oracle Cloud EPM, Thermo Fisher seeks to boost productivity, enable more strategic planning, and support its 3,000 finance and accounting users more effectively.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess Matador Resources (MTDR) After Its Recent Share Price Rebound
feed.summary: Matador Resources (MTDR) has seen a recent share price rebound, although its long-term performance is mixed. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued by 68.9% at $152.61 per share compared to its current price of $47.39. Additionally, its P/E ratio of 7.54x is considerably lower than the oil and gas industry average and Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 17.12x, further indicating a potential undervaluation based on current metrics.

feed.title: Kroger’s New CEO Greg Foran Refocuses Growth Story After Albertsons Deal
feed.summary: Kroger has appointed Greg Foran, former Walmart U.S. chief, as its new CEO and board member. This leadership change comes after the failed merger with Albertsons, shifting investor focus to Kroger's existing assets and digital capabilities. Foran's experience is expected to influence strategic decisions regarding capital allocation, store investments, and potential smaller acquisitions in a competitive grocery market.

feed.title: ASTS vs. ANET: Which Connectivity Stock is the Better Buy Today?
feed.summary: This article compares AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) and Arista Networks (ANET), two key players in the connectivity sector. While AST SpaceMobile is building a global cellular broadband network in space and has seen significant price performance, it faces high operating costs and competition, leading to a Zacks Rank of "Strong Sell." Arista Networks, on the other hand, offers a broad range of data center and campus Ethernet switches, boasts strong revenue growth, superior profitability, and holds a "Hold" rating, making it the better investment option according to the analysis.

feed.title: Texas Pacific Land faces earnings test amid energy pivot to AI
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land (TPL) is set to release its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, with investors closely watching its pivot into data center infrastructure to offset challenges in its core oil and gas royalty business. Analysts expect mixed results, with revenue growth but a significant quarter-over-quarter earnings decline due to depressed Permian Basin oil prices. The company's future success hinges on its ability to secure data center contracts and manage the impact of low natural gas prices on its water royalty income, especially given its high valuation driven by enthusiasm for its strategic shift.

feed.title: What Catalysts Are Rewriting The Ocado Group (LSE:OCDO) Narrative After Target Cuts And Kroger Shift
feed.summary: Ocado Group's fair value estimate has been slightly increased from £2.71 to £2.79 per share, aligning more closely with analyst expectations like JPMorgan's £2.90 target. This adjustment reflects updated assumptions on revenue growth and cost of capital, indicating a recalibration of valuation rather than a complete overhaul. The article discusses both bullish and bearish perspectives on Ocado's future, considering its core technology, retail operations, and recent developments with Kroger.

feed.title: Evergy earnings on deck: Can growth outlook justify valuation?
feed.summary: Evergy Inc. is due to report fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts expecting 54 cents per share on $1.41 billion in revenue. Investors are keenly awaiting the utility's updated long-term growth outlook, particularly concerning the data center construction boom in Kansas and Missouri, which could significantly impact future electricity demand. The company's valuation, currently trading near the industry average, hinges on its ability to demonstrate actionable growth strategies and manage regulatory challenges, especially after a recent earnings miss and a downgrade from UBS.

feed.title: Evolve Private Wealth Names Morgan Stanley Advisor Brett Cohen as Managing Partner
feed.summary: Evolve Private Wealth has appointed Brett Cohen, a former Morgan Stanley advisor who managed approximately $800 million in client assets, as a Managing Partner. This strategic hire expands Evolve's national footprint with a new New York office, bringing the firm's total assets under management to over $2.5 billion and increasing its staff to 22 employees. Cohen's move highlights a growing trend of advisors seeking independent firms that offer long-term flexibility and client-aligned growth opportunities for sophisticated wealth management.

feed.title: Verizon to speak at Barclays Communications and Content Symposium on February 24
feed.summary: Tony Skiadas, Verizon's executive vice president and chief financial officer, is scheduled to speak at the Barclays Communications and Content Symposium on Tuesday, February 24, at 8:00 a.m. ET. The session will be webcast and accessible via Verizon's Investor Relations website. Verizon, a leading communications technology company, generated $138.2 billion in revenues in 2025.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: Kura Oncology (KURA) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
feed.summary: Kura Oncology (KURA) is expected to report a significant year-over-year decline in earnings and lower revenues for Q4 2025. The company is projected to post a quarterly loss of $0.72 per share, with revenues around $29.81 million. The Zacks Earnings ESP indicates a potential negative surprise, making it difficult to predict an earnings beat.

feed.title: Bank of Montreal (BMO) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) is anticipated to report an increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended January 2026, with Wall Street expecting quarterly earnings of $2.39 per share and revenues of $6.63 billion. While the Zacks Earnings ESP suggests no immediate analyst divergence, the company has a strong track record of beating EPS estimates over the past year. Investors should consider these factors as BMO prepares to release its earnings report on February 25.

feed.title: StandardAero, Inc. (SARO) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: StandardAero, Inc. (SARO) is projected to report increased earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an expected 108.3% year-over-year EPS growth to $0.25 and 11% revenue increase to $1.56 billion. Despite a positive Earnings ESP of +5.05%, the company's Zacks Rank #5 makes it difficult to definitively predict an earnings beat. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond just the earnings surprise history, as SARO has only beaten EPS estimates once in the last four quarters.

feed.title: Salesforce.com (CRM) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Salesforce.com (CRM) is projected to report increased earnings and revenues for the quarter ended January 2026. While the Zacks Consensus Estimate for EPS is $3.03, the Earnings ESP is slightly negative at -0.05%, suggesting analysts are leaning bearish. Despite a strong Zacks Rank #2 and a history of beating past EPS estimates, the combination makes predicting an earnings beat for CRM difficult.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: APA (APA) Q4 Earnings Expected to Decline
feed.summary: APA (APA) is projected to report a year-over-year decline in Q4 2025 earnings and revenue, with analysts forecasting $0.62 EPS and $1.92 billion in revenue. Despite a slightly positive Earnings ESP of +0.28%, the company's Zacks Rank of #4 makes a definitive earnings beat difficult to predict. Investors are advised to consider other factors alongside these estimates, as Matador Resources (MTDR) in the same industry also faces similar challenges with its upcoming earnings report.

feed.title: Pinnacle West (PNW) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Pinnacle West (PNW) is anticipated to report an increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ending December 2025, with expectations of $0.05 per share, an increase of 183.3% year-over-year. However, the consensus EPS estimate has been revised 500% lower over the last 30 days, and the company has a negative Earnings ESP of -4.76%. While the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank of #2, the combination of factors makes a positive earnings surprise difficult to predict, suggesting investors should consider other factors along with the company's past performance in beating estimates.

feed.title: Lowe's (LOW) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Lowe's (LOW) is expected to announce a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ending January 2026. The company is projected to post quarterly earnings of $1.95 per share, up 1%, with revenues reaching $20.36 billion, a 9.8% increase. Despite a positive Earnings ESP, Lowe's current Zacks Rank of #4 makes a definitive prediction of an earnings beat difficult.

feed.title: Tech Bytes: Life360 expands Uber tie-up with in-app ride tracking and teen account integration
feed.summary: Life360 has announced a deeper integration with Uber, allowing users to link Uber accounts, including teen accounts, directly within the Life360 app. This new partnership will enable real-time tracking of Uber trips and the ability to request rides, broadening Life360's functionality beyond location sharing to focus on family coordination and potentially boost user engagement. While the market responded modestly, the move is seen as strategically beneficial for both companies by embedding services within overlapping demographics, particularly those with teenagers.

feed.title: EQT Chief Calls for Reforms After Blowout Natural Gas Spot Prices During Winter Storm Fern
feed.summary: EQT Corp. CEO Toby Rice is advocating for reforms in the natural gas industry and stronger permitting processes for pipelines following Winter Storm Fern, which he identified as a critical reminder of natural gas infrastructure's importance. Rice highlighted that despite massive spot price blowouts during the storm, EQT's assets performed well, and pipelines like MVP were essential but maxed out. He urges policymakers to facilitate pipeline construction to ensure energy system reliability.

feed.title: Why Sysco is tagging 10,000 local foods for diners across the U.S. and Canada
feed.summary: Sysco is launching a "Home Grown by Sysco" pilot program to highlight approximately 10,000 locally sourced and artisan products on its Sysco Shop platform. The pilot will begin on February 24 across select regions in the U.S. and Canada, with a target for U.S. expansion within 12 months. This initiative is part of Sysco's sustainability strategy to strengthen local supply chains and respond to growing diner demand for locally sourced foods.

feed.title: Home Grown by Sysco pilot program launches in 3 U.S. regions, locally sourced program includes parts of Canada
feed.summary: Sysco Corporation has launched a pilot "Home Grown by Sysco" program to expand the availability of locally sourced foods and artisan products to customers across three U.S. regions and parts of Canada. The program aims to make approximately 10,000 locally sourced products easily identifiable on Sysco's platforms and plans to expand across the entire U.S. within the next year. This initiative is part of Sysco's broader sustainability strategy, responding to increased customer demand for local products and supporting small businesses and independent restaurants.

feed.title: New Keysight tools tackle bandwidth bottlenecks in AI data centers
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies has introduced new scale-up validation solutions aimed at addressing bandwidth, latency, and interoperability bottlenecks in AI data centers. These solutions support various high-speed interconnect standards including UAlink™200G, PCIe ® 7.0, PCIe ® 6.0, PCIe ® 5.0, and CXL™ 3, facilitating the deployment of efficient AI infrastructure. The offerings include specialized software and hardware for testing and compliance, enabling faster validation and reduced integration risks for next-generation AI accelerators and network devices.
